mechanism_narrative: A lower level of the effective federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns following low FEDFUNDS do not outperform high FEDFUNDS periods with t-test p < 0.05, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over a rolling three-month window, labor market deterioration signals negative earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances, or the spread between triggered and baseline returns is insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Jointly high retail sales (RSAFS) and falling industrial production (INDPRO) indicate demand-supply divergence that squeezes margins and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The bivariate combination does not improve directional accuracy over INDPRO alone (McNemar test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days because demand surprises re-rate cyclical earnings.
failure_condition: RSAFS growth fails to predict positive returns during NBER-dated recoveries (walk-forward IC < 0).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) month-over-month signals improving manufacturing profitability and operating leverage, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO shocks do not coincide with positive 5d returns in > 55% of instances over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising payrolls (PAYEMS) and falling unemployment (UNRATE) confirm a labor market recovery that boosts consumption expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal does not outperform PAYEMS alone in directional accuracy (difference in hit rate < 5 percentage points not significant).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.2 million annualized signals weakening residential investment and negative wealth effects, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST < 1200 episodes produce positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive surprises in real GDP (GDPC1) growth relative to the prior quarter signal stronger aggregate demand than priced by equity markets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 surprises fail to generate positive 60d returns in > 55% of instances.
